compiled_state.description:
# 1.0 release hygiene: dependabot, classifier bump, stability-promise flip, RC skip (tag signing: explicit skip)

## Why

Final mechanical artifacts for the 1.0 declaration, sized to what 2026 peers actually do — which REVERSES two earlier assumptions. (1) Signed git tags are no longer convergent: of CPython/pip/ruff/uv/pytest/httpx, zero sign new release tags in 2026 (CPython dropped GPG at 3.14 per PEP 761; ruff/uv/httpx use lightweight tags); the baseline is annotated tags + GitHub immutable releases + PEP 740 attestations — all already in place here. (2) RC trains are not convergent: pydantic v2 shipped beta→final in 13 days with zero rcs; attrs/structlog skipped 1.0 entirely; the load-bearing artifact is the written compatibility policy, which `docs/api-stability.md` already is.

## What

- `.github/dependabot.yml`: `github-actions` ecosystem ONLY, `interval: monthly`, one catch-all group. The `cooldown` key is **intentionally omitted** — it is in production use in pip's own config (https://github.com/pypa/pip/blob/main/.github/dependabot.yml), but its schema status was disputed in review and its value here is marginal (it only delays update PRs a few days); omitting it removes any dependence on a contested feature. Revisit trigger: update-PR noise. Record this omission decision as one line in the file header.
- **Mirror-interaction decision (recorded in the file header + docs/releasing.md):** this config shape is the pip/pydantic GitHub-native pattern — NOT what Gerrit peers do (they run bespoke bots pushing bumps as Gerrit changes: Wikimedia LibraryUpgrader, OpenStack requirements automation). Since Dependabot's output is a GitHub PR and PRs cannot merge here, Dependabot PRs are ADVISORY NOTIFICATIONS: the maintainer reads the diff, lands the equivalent bump via a Gerrit change, and closes the PR — Dependabot is another shepherded patch author under the PR-as-intake posture. The lockdown workflow exempts `dependabot[bot]` — owned by story **nonsecular-arthritic-sidewinder** (artifact: `.github/workflows/lockdown.yml`, job-level actor guard); this file's header names that workflow file explicitly. Rejected alternatives, one line each in the header: bespoke Gerrit-pushing bot (peer pattern; overkill for a monthly actions-only stream — captured as idea misogynic-cerulean-goldfish); alerts-only with no version-update PRs (loses the ready-made diff; security alerts fire regardless and remain on). No `pip` ecosystem entry — version updates act on pins/lockfiles; rebar's unpinned `>=` core deps give them nothing but noise (rationale in header). Add a `uv` block only if a `uv.lock` is ever committed.
- Staged for the v1.0.0 release change: bump `Development Status :: 4 - Beta` → `5 - Production/Stable` (pyproject classifier) and flip the pre-1.0 caveat in `docs/api-stability.md` so the stability matrix becomes the operative SemVer promise. **Durable staging record (in-repo, not a ticket promise):** `docs/releasing.md` gains a "1.0 declaration" subsection stating exactly these two edits land in the v1.0.0 release change itself, so the requirement survives independent of any tracker.
- `docs/releasing.md` decision records:
  - Tag signing: SKIPPED, with rationale (2026 peer baseline; attestations cover artifact provenance) and revisit triggers (workflow compromise entering the threat model, or a downstream consumer asking to verify tags). Confirm annotated tags remain the practice and immutable releases stay on.
  - RC: SKIPPED (the recorded decision). Rationale: an RC only yields data if someone runs it; with no external adopters, `--pre` installs ≈ 0 and `1.0.1` is the de-facto rc. Revisit trigger: an external adopter volunteering to test. Conditional constraint recorded alongside (today's automation is RC-unsafe): `release.yml` fires on ALL `v*` tags, passes `--latest` unconditionally to `gh release create`, and runs the MCP-registry publish job — a hypothetical `v1.0.0rc1` tag would be marked Latest AND published to the MCP registry. The note must state: no rc-form tag may be pushed unless release.yml first gains a prerelease guard (detect `rc` in the tag → `--prerelease`, not `--latest`; skip the MCP-registry job). PEP 440 spelling (`1.0.0rc1`, not `-rc.1`) used in the constraint text. This story does NOT modify release.yml.
  - Deferral list as non-goals: SBOM/license-scan tooling beyond GitHub's dependency-graph export; Renovate (see idea misogynic-cerulean-goldfish); a lockfile for optional extras (revisit post-1.0).
- Verify at execution: `git tag -l 'v*'` shows no pre-existing rc-form tags contradicting the note.

## Scope

IN: `.github/dependabot.yml`; docs/releasing.md decision records (tag signing, RC skip + guard constraint, 1.0-declaration staging subsection, deferrals).
OUT: modifying release.yml; executing the release (the classifier bump + api-stability flip are EXECUTED in the 1.0 release change, not here — here they are only recorded as staged); contract-freeze code work (exit codes / error envelope / MCP schema pin — tracked elsewhere); SBOM tooling; CI matrix changes (Windows explicitly out — classifiers claim POSIX/macOS, consistent today).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `dependabot.yml` committed — prove: `test -f .github/dependabot.yml`; `grep -c "package-ecosystem" .github/dependabot.yml` = 1; `grep -q "github-actions" .github/dependabot.yml`; `grep -q "interval: monthly" .github/dependabot.yml`; `grep -q 'patterns' .github/dependabot.yml`; `grep -qv "cooldown" .github/dependabot.yml` (key omitted) and the header comment records the omission decision — prove: `grep -qi "cooldown" .github/dependabot.yml` matches only in a comment line.
- [ ] Advisory-PR flow documented — prove: `grep -qi "advisory" .github/dependabot.yml` and `grep -q "lockdown.yml" .github/dependabot.yml` (header names the exemption artifact); `grep -qi "gerrit" .github/dependabot.yml`.
- [ ] Dependabot accepts the config — prove: after push, the Insights → Dependency graph → Dependabot view shows the update job scheduled with no config-parse error.
- [ ] docs/releasing.md decisions present — prove: `grep -qi "tag signing" docs/releasing.md`; `grep -q "1.0.0rc1" docs/releasing.md`; `grep -q -- "--latest" docs/releasing.md`; `grep -qi "SBOM" docs/releasing.md`; `grep -qi "renovate" docs/releasing.md`.
- [ ] 1.0-declaration staging subsection present — prove: `grep -qi "Production/Stable" docs/releasing.md` and `grep -q "api-stability" docs/releasing.md` (the subsection names both staged edits and their landing vehicle).
- [ ] No rc-form tags exist — prove: `git tag -l | grep -c rc` = 0.
compiled_state.title: 1.0 release hygiene: dependabot, classifier bump, stability-promise flip, RC decision
